title: Ravager (Centurion-class)

{{Quote|The hunger that fills this vessel… it is power… but it consumes without end.|Tobin|audio=Tobin1.flac|<ref name="TSL" />}}
The '''''Ravager''''' was a [[Centurion-class battlecruiser/Legends|''Centurion''-class battlecruiser]] used by the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. In the [[Battle of Malachor V|final battle]] of the war, it was all but destroyed by the activation of the [[Mass Shadow Generator/Legends|Mass Shadow Generator]] and lay in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] until [[Darth Nihilus]] took control of it. The [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to hold the ship together with repairs only just making the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] habitable for Nihilus and his crew of [[Nihilus slave|Force slaves]].

After receiving a tip-off about a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Conclave at Katarr|Conclave]], the ''Ravager'' was taken to [[Katarr/Legends|Katarr]] where Nihilus [[Conclave at Katarr|drained the planet of life]]. He kept [[Visas Marr]], one of the native [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]], as his [[Shadow Hand]]. Nihilus later allied with [[General/Legends|General]] [[Vaklu]], the leader of the [[Onderon separatists|separatists]] on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], so that he could use the planet as a staging ground for the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. However, the plans were thwarted by [[Meetra Surik]] by her assistance in stopping the separatists and killing Vaklu, leaving [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Tobin (Human)|Tobin]] as Nihilus' contact. Nihilus then took the ''Ravager'' to [[Telos IV]] to feed on the Force energies of the planet.

In an attempt to protect Telos, Meetra Surik, Visas Marr, and [[Canderous Ordo]] boarded the ''Ravager'' to kill Nihilus and destroy the ship. They planted [[Proton grenade/Legends|proton charges]] at weak points in the [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship's]] superstructure before dueling Nihilus on the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]], [[Death/Legends|killing]] him. The strike team then finally destroyed the ''Ravager'' when they detonated the charges they had set.

==Description==
{{Quote|This ship… is it his weakness? It should not exist, yet it cruises the darkness between the stars. He tore it from the mass shadows of Malachor, along with his fleet… that is a measure of his power.|Tobin|audio=Tobin3.flac|<ref name="TSL" />}}
The ''Ravager'' was technically [[Realspace/Legends|unspaceworthy]] and, under normal circumstances, would be impossible to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] or live within. Its hull was broken and blasted open with holes, dents, and other places where it was torn apart by [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] guns. Damage had been caused when it was crushed by the [[Mass Shadow Generator/Legends|Mass Shadow Generator]] and further strained when drawn out of that [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] field. The infrastructure was exposed in many places where the outer plating had been torn off. Parts of the vessel were missing and the original [[Color/Legends|color]] had long faded, replaced with the burn marks from its last battle. Sparks leaped across the hull at intervals and pulsated along the surface. The [[Starship/Legends|starship]] was open to space in many places, with [[Darth Nihilus]] allowing the [[Particle shield/Legends|particle shields]] to contain only a minimal [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. However, despite these problems, the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] proved itself powerful in battle. The ship was held together by Nihilus's power and will, making it comparable to the ancient tales of ghost ships held on many [[Planet/Legends|worlds]].<ref name="TSL" />

[[File:Visasmeditationchamber.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Visas Marr's meditation chamber]]
Within, the ship was in no better condition than the exterior. The halls had not seen maintenance in [[Standard year/Legends|years]], with dust collecting across the dead [[Computer/Legends|computer]] readouts and the empty mess halls. The lighting was dim and yellowed, near-orange in places, and then only where the lighting still worked. Sparks flew from the walls and exposed piping and wiring as they too malfunctioned and shorted out. The worn metal floor was rusted as were the control panels on the walls. Bare power stations stood uncared for in years. The halls, with a rectangular or rhombic cross-section, wound through empty rooms, several leading out into nothing but space beyond broken walls. However, most of the doors still functioned and one small [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]] was in usable order. Armories still held supplies, though some of their contents had fallen into decay through years of disuse. [[Barracks/Legends|Barracks]] contained beds and quarters although the beds were frayed and the lights were almost out. There was a single, inactive [[Missile/Legends|missile]] bay with one missile still in the tube.<ref name="TSL" />

[[Visas Marr]]'s cell was carved out of the rear starboard hull of the ship and was somewhat unique. It was dull and dim like the rest of the ship and consisted of a small bedroom quarter with two spacious living quarters, bare of any furniture except a table and several chairs bolted to the floor around it. A [[Meditation/Legends|meditation]] room was adjoined to that and was unique in that it was roughly spherical and not squared like the rest of the ship. Rectangular pillars bent outwards along the walls, with blue writing adorning the sides that glowed at [[Time/Legends|times]]. Torn banners hung between these.<ref name="TSL" />

The [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] was also distinctive from the remainder of the ship. Behind the bridge proper was a command room whose middle was entirely dedicated to the deactivated computers, surrounded by dust-covered seats. Behind that were six other large rooms arranged in a tight, rectangular "C" shape, connected to the main level via a large, hexagonal elevator that still operated. The bridge was extremely long, with indentations along either side of the main pathway in which [[Nihilus slave|barely-living zombies]], [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] of Nihilus's will, worked mindlessly at the ships controls. On the side of these were two other walkways. The bridge was lit dimly with red lighting and two glow lamps, shaped like thorns, rose to either side of the beginning and end of the center walkway. The bridge was mostly lit by the huge windows above it which made up most of the ceiling, though these opened into space in most places, with the infrastructure of the ship showing everywhere. The center of the ceiling was made of metal with most of the wiring and components hanging down precariously. A bare, clamshell-shaped command station stood at the end in front of a large [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]], void of any computer terminals.<ref name="TSL" />

==History==
[[File:Ravagerbridge.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The bridge of the ''Ravager'']]
{{Quote|This ship… it is a graveyard of a terrible battle. Everything on it slowly dies as long as he hungers.|Tobin|audio=Tobin2.flac|<ref name="TSL" />}}
The ''Ravager'' was originally one of a fleet of [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] that saw service during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. The well-known ship was destroyed, along with most of its brethren, at the [[Battle of Malachor V]]. It had first been rendered useless by Mandalorian guns and then impossible to salvage when the Mass Shadow generator was activated, crushing or trapping everything within its reach in the sudden surge of intense gravity. There the ship lay for a time, floating about [[Malachor V]] when Darth Nihilus took it for his use and imbued it with the power to fly and destroy in his need to feed his insatiable appetite for power as he escaped imprisonment on the world.<ref name="TSL" />

After Nihilus became aware that a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Conclave at Katarr|Conclave]] was to be held on [[Katarr/Legends|Katarr]], due to information spread by [[Jedi historian]] [[Atris]] to draw him out,<ref name="CTR">{{LucasArts|url=games/swkotor_sithlords|text=''Chronicles of the Old Republic''|int=Chronicles of the Old Republic}}</ref> the ''Ravager'' soon arrived on the scene. Consequently, the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Conclave at Katarr|destroyed all life]] on the world, save [[Visas Marr|one]]. Now bearing two lives not bereft of soul, as the twelve pilots were Force slaves whose life energy was slowly being drained to sustain Nihilus, the ''Ravager'' traveled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], searching for worlds on which its master could feed. As it traveled, Nihilus gathered several [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith troopers]] to defend his flagship.<ref name="TSL" />

As they traversed the stars, Visas, the [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] Nihilus had spared on Katarr, reported seeing a great Force-user, but Nihilus bid her no mind. However, eventually she was sent to discover the Jedi and bring them to the Sith Lord to be consumed by his growing hunger.<ref name="TSL" />

After noticing a small insurgency on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], Nihilus took the ''Ravager'' to the world to make a pact with [[General/Legends|General]] [[Vaklu]]'s [[Onderon separatists|faction]], offering to aid them and give them power if they would do as he dictated. After making the deal, Nihilus proved to be too powerful for Vaklu's military to control, but by then it was too late. In fear of the Dark Lord, they remained loyal.<ref name="TSL" />

[[File:Ravagerboom.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The ''Ravager'', escorted by ''Interdictor''-class cruisers, explodes at Telos IV.]]
That loyalty proved true when [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Tobin (Human)|Tobin]] of the Onderonian military heard from [[Kreia]] in the year [[3951 BBY]] that a [[Telosian Jedi Academy|Jedi Academy]] could be found on [[Telos IV]] and alerted his dark master. He was unaware, however, that it was a trap. Nihilus, finding only Atris, would drain all life from the world in a last-ditch effort to save himself and be soon thereafter killed, thus killing both Atris and Nihilus in one stroke. However, neither of them counted on [[Meetra Surik]] who, after defeating Atris in a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]], was on Telos at the time of Nihilus's [[Battle of Telos IV|attack]] on the world.<ref name="TSL" />

She and the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] she had befriended concocted a plan to destroy the ''Ravager'' and its master. A secret detachment consisting of Meetra Surik, Visas Marr, [[Canderous Ordo|Mandalore the Preserver]], and several Neo-Crusaders boarded the ship, which was being escorted by [[Interdictor-class cruiser|''Interdictor''-class cruisers]], and planted several [[Proton grenade/Legends|proton charges]] along weakened areas in the outer hull. They knew they could not leave Nihilus alive, however, as he could have had the power to prevent the ship from being destroyed as he had been doing for years.<ref name="TSL" />

{{GameLS|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords}}
As Surik made her way to the bridge, she met the shell of a man that was Tobin; a slave to Nihilus who slowly drained away the Colonel's life leaving him with only enough to survive. After she convinced him that serving Nihilus would not help Onderon and that the planet would likely be just another place for Nihilus to feed, the Colonel agreed to set off the charges for her. Surik continued to the bridge where she confronted the Sith Lord, with the help of Visas and Mandalore. In one stroke, Surik killed Nihilus and freed Visas from her link to the Dark Lord. With that completed, they detonated the charges, destroying the ship once and for all.<ref name="TSL" />
{{Endgame}}

==Commanders and crew==
During the Mandalorian Wars, the command of the ''Ravager'' fell to the Republic Navy. Darth Nihilus then assumed command of the ship when he recovered it from the orbit of Malachor V after the Mass Shadow Generator was activated. The running of the vessel itself fell to a skeleton crew of Force slaves under Nihilus's control with security provided by Sith troopers.<ref name="TSL" /> When Nihilus attacked Katarr, he brought Visas Marr to his ship and took her as his Shadow Hand.<ref name="Unseen, Unheard">{{Tales|24|Unseen, Unheard}}</ref><ref name="TSL" /> She was given her own quarters on the ship complete with a meditation chamber for her to center herself in. When Nihilus sent her to find Meetra Surik, Marr did not know where the ''Ravager'' would be, thus preventing Surik from finding Nihilus. After [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Talia (Onderon)|Talia]] arrested Vaklu, Tobin became Nihilus's contact and ended up on the ''Ravager'', where the Sith Lord gradually drained his life. Surik convinced Tobin to detonate the charges that would destroy the ship, killing him in the process.<ref name="TSL" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Ravagercrashesintocitadel.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The ''Ravager'' crashes into Citadel Station.]]
Like the [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']], the appearance of the ''Ravager'' is similar to the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]] of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' films.<ref name="KTR">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref><ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref><ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref><ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref>

When the Mandalorian crew assaulted the ''Ravager'' and Mandalore entered it, the whole scene may be regarded as an homage to the raid on the [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']], with Mandalore thus "impersonating" [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].<ref name="TSL" /><ref name="ANH" />

Originally, during the game ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', after Surik and her companions defeated Darth Nihilus and did not set the last proton core, the ''Ravager'' would spiral into the Citadel Station, similar to the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']]{{'s}} crash during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], and destroy it. This can be seen in secret movies in the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|Prima Guide]] DVD.<ref name="POGG">[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref>
